relations.note: Defines how the general palette hierarchy applies within charts.
Defines approved tertiary surface applications for callouts.
Narrows Coral's role within the tertiary accent tier.
Adds an email-specific default against Coral use while allowing brief-driven exceptions.

quotes: Apply the palette in the **primary > secondary > tertiary ratio** shown in the guide: blues and golds dominate; tertiary colors are accents and surfaces.

rules.intent: Preserve the intended prominence of primary, secondary, and tertiary brand colors.
rules.rule_text: Apply the palette in primary > secondary > tertiary hierarchy: blues and golds dominate, while tertiary colors serve as accents and surfaces.

quotes: **Headlines/headings:** Brand Blue (or White on dark/gradient backgrounds).
**Body text:** Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds (with increased line height, §1.2).
**Headlines/headings:** Brand Blue (or White on dark/gradient backgrounds).
**Body text:** Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds (with increased line height, §1.2).

rules.intent: Maintain branded text hierarchy and readability across light and dark surfaces.
rules.rule_text: Use headline.color = Brand Blue (#00529b) for headlines and headings, with White on dark or gradient backgrounds. Use body.color = Graphite (#212121) on light backgrounds and White on dark backgrounds; dark-background body text also observes the body minimum line height of 150%.

quotes: **Links [GENERAL]:** Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark.
**Links [GENERAL]:** Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark.

rules.intent: Keep links consistently recognizable and legible across background treatments.
rules.rule_text: Links use body.link.color = Brand Blue (#00529b) on light backgrounds and White on dark backgrounds, together with body.link.text_decoration = underline.

quotes: **Charts/about:blank** Brand Blue + Sky Blue dominant; **Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures**; gray for placebo.
**Charts/about:blank** Brand Blue + Sky Blue dominant; **Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures**; gray for placebo.

rules.intent: Create a consistent chart hierarchy while reserving warm colors for meaningful emphasis.
rules.rule_text: Charts use chart.data.colors = Brand Blue (#00529b) with Sky Blue (#358CCB) as the dominant data colors. Sunshine (#faa31b) and Gold (#ffc60a) are strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures, and chart.placebo.color = neutral gray is used for placebo.

quotes: **Callout surfaces:** Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts).
**Callout surfaces:** Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts).

rules.intent: Differentiate data and text-heavy callouts through approved surface treatments.
rules.rule_text: Data callout surfaces use callout.secondary.fill = Light Blue (#E6F0F9) with opacity.callout.light_blue_tint_70 = 70%. Text-heavy callouts use callout.text_heavy.fill = Golden Sand (#FEF1C8) with callout.text_heavy.fill.opacity = 30%.

quotes: **Coral:** sparing tertiary accent only — never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data.
**Coral:** sparing tertiary accent only — never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data.

rules.intent: Prevent a tertiary accent from competing with primary brand colors or implying data significance.
rules.rule_text: Coral (#F26A38) is restricted to sparing tertiary accents and must never be used for primary CTAs, headlines, or data.

quotes: **[GENERAL]:** avoid Coral in email sections unless the brief calls for it.

rules.intent: Keep email palettes focused while permitting brief-driven exceptions.
rules.rule_text: Avoid Coral (#F26A38) in email sections unless the brief specifically calls for it.

quotes: The logo icon's **70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a must be maintained in all instances**
The logo icon's **70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a must be maintained in all instances** (logo, and when the mark is used as a pattern/graphic).

rules.intent: Preserve the approved appearance of the logo mark across all applications.
rules.rule_text: Maintain logo.mark.opacity = 70% for the Pantone 7548 C / Gold (#ffc60a) logo icon in every instance, including the logo and any use of the mark as a pattern or graphic.
